proposal_line: A producer's note appears on your monitor: "Tomorrow's schedule is open. Pick the first activity to set the tone for Day 2."
invalid_choice_text: Pick one of the listed activities.
narrative_lines: The note blinks once, then fades. The house below continues its quiet evening, unaware of what you have chosen for them.

options.title: Cooking Lesson with Ren
options.menu_text: Ren teaches someone his signature dish — intimacy through shared focus in the kitchen.
narrative_lines: A casual morning lesson. Ren finds a reason to stand close, to correct a grip on a knife, to taste what someone else has made.

options.title: Morning Run with Daichi
options.menu_text: Daichi invites someone to train on the patio — connection through physical effort and shared silence.
narrative_lines: Daichi does not offer encouragement. He just starts, and whoever follows him discovers how much can be said without words.

options.title: Editing Session with Aera
options.menu_text: Aera reviews footage with someone — intimacy through being truly seen on screen.
narrative_lines: Aera pulls up clips from the first day. Watching yourself through someone else's eyes is either flattering or terrifying. Sometimes both.

proposal_line: A producer's note: "Day 3 needs a centerpiece. What kind of group energy do you want to create?"
invalid_choice_text: Pick one of the listed events.
narrative_lines: The note disappears. Somewhere in the house, Ren is already reaching for a cutting board, Aera is opening her laptop, and Mio is looking for a deck of cards. They do not yet know which version of today they are about to live.

options.title: Themed Dinner Party
options.menu_text: Ren proposes a cuisine theme. Everyone cooks together. Collaboration stress reveals who leads, who follows, who resists.
narrative_lines: A dinner where the menu is a statement and the cooking is a negotiation. Ren sets the standard, and everyone else decides how close they want to stand to it.

options.title: Collaborative Art Project
options.menu_text: Aera proposes a short film or photo series. Everyone contributes. Creative differences surface fast.
narrative_lines: Aera sets up the shot. Everyone else decides what they are willing to show — and what they would rather keep out of frame.

options.title: Game Night
options.menu_text: Mio picks a game. Competition, alliances, and the revelation of who is secretly cutthroat.
narrative_lines: Mio finds a game that rewards charm and risk. By the second round, everyone has shown something they did not plan to.

proposal_line: A producer's note: "Day 4. The house is restless. Time to introduce friction. What kind of pressure do you want to apply?"
invalid_choice_text: Pick one of the listed disruptions.
narrative_lines: The note holds on screen a beat longer than usual. Below, the house is mid-breakfast, unaware that the rules are about to change.

options.title: Power Outage
options.menu_text: The power cuts out at dusk. The house splits into two candlelit groups. Forced proximity reveals what daylight hides.
narrative_lines: No screens. No escape. Just faces in candlelight and whatever people say when they forget they are being watched.

options.title: Truth Circle
options.menu_text: Mio proposes a late-night question game. Everyone must answer honestly. The price of admission is a secret.
narrative_lines: The rules are simple: answer or leave the circle. No one leaves. But the questions get harder fast.

options.title: One Person Leaves for the Day
options.menu_text: One housemate is called away. The remaining three navigate the absence — who misses them, who thrives without them.
narrative_lines: The front door closes behind them. The house exhales. And the three remaining discover who they are when the fourth is not in the room.

proposal_line: A producer's note: "Day 5. After the friction of yesterday, the house needs a reset. Or does it? Your call."
invalid_choice_text: Pick one of the listed activities.
narrative_lines: The note is shorter than usual. The producer is running out of patience — or running out of script. Either way, the house is yours to shape.

options.title: Paired Cooking Challenge
options.menu_text: Two teams of two, cooking head-to-head. You choose the teams. Competition reveals loyalties and surprising chemistry.
narrative_lines: Two cutting boards, two stoves, four people who are not sure whether they are competing with each other or for each other.

options.title: Morning Walk in Pairs
options.menu_text: Two pairs walk at different times. Side-by-side walking lowers defenses. You choose who walks with whom.
narrative_lines: The street is quiet. Walking next to someone is different from sitting across from them. The conversation changes when you are both looking forward.

options.title: Aera Screens the Footage
options.menu_text: Aera shows edited clips from the week. Everyone watches themselves through her eyes. Reactions range from recognition to discomfort.
narrative_lines: Aera has been watching all along. Now everyone else gets to see what she saw — and what she chose not to show.

proposal_line: A producer's note: "Last night. Tomorrow they leave. Make it count."
selection_prompt: Choose how the last night unfolds:
invalid_choice_text: Pick one of the listed options.
narrative_lines: The note stays on screen. Below, the house moves through its last full day, and every gesture already feels like a memory.

options.title: Final Group Dinner
options.menu_text: Everyone cooks together one last time. Every shared task, every glance across the counter, carries the weight of goodbye.
narrative_lines: The kitchen is warm. The chopping is slower. No one mentions that this is the last time, but everyone knows.

options.title: Confessional Round
options.menu_text: Two private interviews. You choose who gets the spotlight. What they say in the chair is between them and the camera.
narrative_lines: The confessional is a small room with one chair and one lens. Some people perform. Some people break. Both are worth watching.

options.title: Stargazing on the Patio
options.menu_text: The whole house drifts outside. No agenda. The conversation slows to the pace of breathing. Goodbyes begin before anyone says the word.
narrative_lines: The patio is narrow and the sky is wide. Someone brings a blanket. No one checks their phone.
